page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-24GB is a KVM-based virtual server belonging to the CLOUDNATIVE family. It features 8 shared vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system is built on the x86_64 architecture and includes 24.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates average overall performance across most multi-threaded workloads, though single-core execution is a notable bottleneck. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core performance aligns with the middle 50% of tested systems. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 821.2 GB/sec, the PassMark memory score is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. The server handles standard tasks like software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving at average speeds, but struggles with heavier workloads, as evidenced by poor text generation speeds on medium-sized 7B LLM models.

Given its shared CPU allocation and lack of dedicated local storage, this server offers a cost-effective resource profile for non-critical, parallelizable workloads. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications that can tolerate shared CPU resources, such as general-purpose web serving, basic database operations, and lightweight background processing. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or memory-intensive applications due to its lower single-core performance and weak memory benchmark scores.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-24GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 8 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 24.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU acceleration, this x86_64 instance relies on shared CPU allocation, making it a budget-friendly option for multi-threaded workloads. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 6189.0 and a static web serving throughput of 6.02 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is weak, with stress-ng single-core results placing in the bottom 10%. Memory performance is also limited, as reflected in a weak PassMark memory score. This server is best suited for general-purpose web serving, basic database operations, and multi-threaded background tasks that do not require dedicated CPU resources or high single-core speed.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-24GB is a KVM virtual server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 24 GB RAM. It offers average multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core and memory benchmarks.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-24GB is an x86_64 virtual server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance.
tagline: A shared-resource virtual server offering eight vCPUs and balanced multi-core performance for budget-conscious general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
24.0 GB of system memory with no bundled local storage
Average multi-core performance for web serving and software compilation
Weak single-core CPU and memory benchmark results
Suitable for budget-friendly, multi-threaded general workloads
